untranslatable_reason: This signal describes a bar construction methodology (dollar-value sampling) rather than entry/exit trading conditions. Dollar value exchanged is not available as an indicator in the allowed list. The signal requires tracking cumulative notional value (price × volume) across bars, which is a data preprocessing step, not a tradeable condition. Translating this would require: (1) a dollar_volume or notional_value indicator, and (2) custom bar aggregation logic outside standard indicator evaluation.
translation_notes: LOPEZ_DRY_0_0 is a regime-detection and bar-construction rule, not a traditional entry/exit signal. It specifies using dollar-value sampling (close × volume aggregation) to normalize bar structure during high-volatility periods. This belongs in the data pipeline/bar construction layer, not in the condition evaluation engine. To implement: aggregate bars by cumulative (close × volume) >= threshold rather than by tick count or volume count.
